general_principle: CLI over MCP for reads (field selection, output piping, documented behavior). MCP for writes where output size does not matter, and services with no CLI equivalent.

searchJiraIssuesUsingJql.optimization: Use fields parameter to select only needed fields, limit maxResults
searchJiraIssuesUsingJql.default_bloat: Includes description, issuetype, priority, created -- roughly 2x payload per issue. Description fields on form-generated tickets can be hundreds of tokens each.

searchConfluenceUsingCql.optimization: Use narrow CQL and limit parameter
searchConfluenceUsingCql.notes: Avoid expand unless you need page body content.

getJiraIssue.optimization: Prefer over broad JQL search when you have the ticket number

gmail_search_messages.optimization: Use specific q filters and low maxResults
gmail_search_messages.notes: Default is 20 results, each with headers, snippet, and metadata. Use q filters: from:, after:, subject:, is:unread.

gmail_read_message.optimization: Prefer over gmail_read_thread when you need one message
gmail_read_message.notes: Thread reads return every message in the conversation.

gcal_list_events.optimization: Use tight timeMin/timeMax bounds and low maxResults
gcal_list_events.notes: Default is 50 results per page. Keep condenseEventDetails: true (default). Setting false adds full attendee lists, attachments, timestamps per event.

WebFetch.optimization: Use a focused prompt parameter to control output size
WebFetch.notes: No maxResults or limit parameters. The prompt is the only knob controlling output size.

WebSearch.notes: Result count is server-controlled with no limit parameter. Avoid chaining WebSearch into multiple WebFetch calls.

cli_examples.issue_view: gh issue view <N> --json title,state (1 line vs 65 default)
cli_examples.pr_view: gh pr view <N> --json title,state (1 line vs ~30 default)

get_issue.optimization: Prefer CLI gh issue view --json for reads
get_issue.notes: Returns ~100 lines (full body, user objects with 15+ URL fields, labels, reactions) vs 1 line from CLI with --json.

list_issues.notes: Returns ~200 lines with per_page: 2. No field filtering available via MCP.

list_tables.notes: verbose: true adds all columns, types, defaults, constraints -- ~20x larger.

execute_sql.optimization: Always LIMIT results, select specific columns
execute_sql.notes: SELECT id, status, created_at returns ~6x less than SELECT * on a typical 16-column table.

list_deployments.optimization: Use since/until timestamps to narrow results
list_deployments.notes: Returns 20 items per page with verbose git metadata (~25 lines each). No limit parameter available.

get_deployment_build_logs.optimization: Use limit parameter for quick checks
get_deployment_build_logs.notes: Defaults to 100 lines. Full 100 only when debugging build failures.

get_runtime_logs.optimization: Filter by level, environment, source, and time window
get_runtime_logs.notes: Defaults to 50 entries (max 1000). Use tight since/until to avoid pulling all log levels.
